description: ALFA_tag — a 13-aa synthetic epitope tag (SRLEEELRRRLTE) recognized by the NbALFA nanobody with ultra-high affinity (Kd = 26 pM), used for protein detection, purification, and imaging.

references.authors: Götzke H, Kilisch M, Martínez-Carranza M, Sograte-Idrissi S, Rajavel A, Schlichthaerle T, Engels N, Jungmann R, Stenmark P, Opazo F, Frey S
references.title: The ALFA-tag is a highly versatile tool for nanobody-based bioscience applications
Discovery and Characterization of an ALFA-Tag-Specific Affinity Resin Optimized for Protein Purification at Low Temperatures in Physiological Buffer
A Light-Activatable Photocaged Variant of the Ultra-High Affinity ALFA-Tag Nanobody

references.authors: Fay A, Kurland AP, Li Z, Monetti M, Johnson JR, Glickman MS
references.title: A split ALFA tag-nanobody system for protein localization and proximity proteomics in mycobacteria

references.authors: Akhuli D, Dhar A, Viji AS, Bhojappa B, Palani S
references.title: ALIBY: ALFA Nanobody-Based Toolkit for Imaging and Biochemistry in Yeast
Application of ALFA-Tagging in the Nematode Model Organisms Caenorhabditis elegans and Pristionchus pacificus

label: ALFA_tag
note: 13-aa synthetic epitope tag SRLEEELRRRLTE. Recognized with sub-nanomolar affinity by NbALFA (Kd = 26 pM). No internal functional sub-elements; the tag acts as a single binding epitope.

provenance.sequence_source: PDB 6I2G chain B, entity 2 ("ALFA-tag peptide", synthetic construct, PSRLEEELRRRLTEP 15 aa; ALFA tag core SRLEEELRRRLTE = positions 1-14, 100% identity over all 13 aa; deposited 2018-11-01, released 2019-09-25; Götzke H et al., The ALFA-tag is a highly versatile tool for nanobody-based bioscience applications, Nat Commun 10:4403, 2019, PMID 31562305, doi:10.1038/s41467-019-12301-7)

functional_claims.label: ALFA tag is bound by the NbALFA nanobody with Kd = 26 pM, one of the highest affinities reported for an epitope tag/binder pair.

value.measurement_method: isothermal titration calorimetry and surface plasmon resonance

source.quote: NbALFA binds ALFA-tag with a Kd of 26 pM

functional_claims.label: Fusion proteins bearing the ALFA tag can be purified by NbALFA-coupled affinity resin; a cold-elutable NbALFACE variant (Kd ~100 nM) enables gentle elution at 4°C under physiological buffer.

source.quote: efficient elution at 4°C in physiological buffer

functional_claims.label: The ALFA tag functions independently of its position (N-terminal, C-terminal, or internal), making it compatible with diverse fusion architectures.
The ALFA tag has been validated in diverse hosts including E. coli, mammalian cells, S. cerevisiae, C. elegans, P. pacificus, and M. tuberculosis.

source.quote: highly versatile tool for nanobody-based bioscience applications

functional_claims.label: The ALFA tag supports immunofluorescence imaging and super-resolution microscopy via NbALFA conjugated to fluorescent dyes or proteins.

value.binder: NbALFA-fluorophore conjugate or NbALFA-fluorescent protein fusion

source.quote: versatile tool for nanobody-based bioscience applications

functional_claims.label: At 13 aa (39 bp coding), the ALFA tag is small enough for CRISPR-mediated knock-in at endogenous loci without disrupting protein function.

source.quote: CRISPR-mediated insertion of the ALFA-tag does not perturb protein function

functional_claims.label: A photocaged NbALFA variant (ALFA-Pb, Tyr42ONBY substitution) reduces affinity to Kd 3.2–7.9 µM in the dark and restores near-wild-type affinity (~183–346 pM) after 365 nm UV irradiation, enabling light-controlled binding to the ALFA tag.

source.quote: photocaged variant with Tyr42ONBY has Kd 3.2–7.9 µM inactive, restores to ~183–346 pM after 365 nm UV irradiation

functional_claims.label: A split ALFA tag/NbALFA system enables proximity proteomics and protein localization studies, demonstrated in mycobacteria using TurboID-NbALFA.

source.quote: split ALFA tag-nanobody system for protein localization and proximity proteomics in mycobacteria
